event_blocks.summary: The talent show segment opens on a brightly lit stage with a vibrant blue and purple backdrop, where performer Kevin Li, clad in a beige jacket and pants over a white t-shirt, stands beside an easel displaying six photos. After introducing himself as Kevin Li, 25, from Los Angeles, he confidently declares his talent is magic, drawing cheers from the audience and an enthusiastic "We love magic!" from Judge 2 (Heidi, woman in yellow top). Kevin shares how watching the show with his family fostered bonding, expressing his hope to share that joy. As uplifting music plays, he begins his act, holding up a "COOL MEMORIES!" card and a Polaroid of a red rose, explaining photos define us and capture memories. He addresses the judges as "Simon, Heidi, Sophia, Howie, Terry and myself," handing photos to Heidi, Judge 3 (Simon, man in dark suit), and Judge 1 (Howie, bald, glasses, black jacket). Heidi smiles and responds "Hello" and "Yes" to his instructions, while Simon reacts with surprise, raising his hand. Kevin instructs Heidi to show her photo, which depicts a hand reaching into a red cup, to everyone before hiding it. He then approaches Heidi, instructing her to hold her index finger, accompanied by a high-pitched sci-fi sound effect, before pointing at her and saying "E.T. phone home." Kevin, seeing a larger screen and smelling something buttery, prompts audience laughter and a "Whoa!" from Judge 3. He asks the judges if they are eating popcorn at a movie theater, eliciting gasps and an "Oh no!" from the audience and Heidi. He then asks Heidi, now addressed as Sophia, to "pretend you're placing a piece of popcorn on my hand," to which she replies, "Oh, that's a popcorn kernel." As a suspenseful hum begins, Kevin instructs, "Watch closely. Don't blink," followed by a loud "whoosh" sound, audience screams, and cheers as Kevin exclaims "Look at that!" and "What a performer!" The judges' table is adorned with Sonic-branded cups, microphones, and red buzzers, and the segment concludes with Kevin Li confidently pointing to his eye, having completed a dramatic interaction that leaves the audience visibly engaged and reacting with shock and amusement.

event_blocks.summary: Kevin Li, dressed in a beige jacket and matching pants over a white t-shirt, captivated the judges and audience with a dramatic performance on center stage, initiating the act by asking Judge 1, "Simon, what do you see inside the box?" to which Simon replied, "Nothing." Kevin Li then instructed, "We'll take the kernel, place it inside... and... heat it up a bit," producing a folded paper with 'CORE MEMORIES' written on it, which he tore into pieces and handed to Judge 2, eliciting a collective "Wow!" and applause from the audience, alongside wide-eyed surprise from all three judges. Transitioning across the stage, Kevin Li removed his jacket to reveal a plain white t-shirt and approached an easel displaying six Polaroid-style photos of children pinned to a brown board, set against a dynamic cityscape backdrop glowing with warm orange and blue hues. He spoke about the importance of memories, suggesting, "Let's rewind the hands of time even more. Judges, let's all go back," prompting a sentimental music cue and his exclamation, "Whoa! It's us! Look how cute you all are! Even Simon!" The judges, including Judge 3, were visibly amused, with Judge 3 laughing heartily along with the audience, while Sonic-branded cups and red buzzers remained on the judges' table. Later, Judge 2 praised Kevin's emotional performance, noting his control and spectacular presence, to which Kevin responded with a simple "Thank you." Judge 1, Simon, commended Kevin's unique and meaningful performance, stating, "You're just good, and you're cool, and you're different... I could tell how much this means to you... And you're slick, and you're vaguest, and you're very us. And I really like you," to which Kevin enthusiastically replied, "Thank you, Simon! Thank you! Thank you!" Judge 3 reflected on Kevin's journey, highlighting the image of a young boy watching America's Got Talent at home and then rising to the occasion on the very stage he watched, as Kevin continued his performance with conviction, eliciting continued surprise, laughter, and curiosity from the judges and animated excitement from the audience, culminating in a wide shot of Kevin Li facing the judges amidst sustained applause and cheers.

On stage, Brynn Cummings, a young girl in a green dress with a white heart pattern, stands confidently beside her pink and white furry puppet perched on a stand. She introduces herself, stating, "and I'm a ventriloquist," which elicits an audible "Ah!" from the surprised audience. event_blocks.summary: Young Brynn Cummings, dressed in a green dress with a white heart pattern, confidently performs on stage with a pink and white furry puppet. The puppet, speaking in a high-pitched voice, expresses a desire to switch from singing to mentalism, surprising Brynn. The audience laughs and cheers as Brynn, addressing the puppet as 'Reed,' attempts to persuade it to sing, but ultimately agrees to the mentalism act and takes the puppet towards the judges' table. Judges 2 and 3 approve as Brynn hands the puppet to Judge 2, who compliments its realism. The puppet expresses nervousness, but Brynn reassures it, suggesting they use a deck of cards. The puppet reacts with surprise and a "Ta-da!" as Judge 2 picks up a red straw, and Brynn instructs Judge 2, 'Heidi,' to cut the deck, accompanied by playful, mysterious music. Later, Brynn, blindfolded, interacts with the puppet, which correctly identifies Sofia's object as an oatmeal cookie from a bag of crumbs, leading to a solo attempt where the puppet guesses Simon's object, an Apple Watch, fittingly. The judges express amazement and approval, with Simon calling Brynn 'so talented, so charming' and predicting she will be a fan favorite for her unique 'mashup' of magic, mentalism, and ventriloquism. The performance concludes with the judges, including Heidi and Howie, seated at their table with Sonic-branded cups, receiving applause from the engaged audience.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Brynn Cummings, a young girl in a green dress with a white heart pattern, confidently on stage operating a pink and white furry puppet. The puppet, speaking in a high-pitched voice, declares, "Big moment. Are you ready for me to queue the music? I don't wanna sing anymore." Brynn, using a slightly lower voice, responds, "What? This is what we've practiced for weeks." The puppet continues, "I've been thinking about it, and it's done, done. They wanna see something else, something new." Brynn then asks, "But we haven't practiced anything else. What's your plan?" The puppet exclaims, "I've got it! Mentalism!" to which Brynn, in the puppet's voice, reacts with a surprised "Mentalism? Wait, what?" The audience laughs and cheers. Brynn, addressing the puppet as "Reed," says, "I don't mind, Reed. Quit playing around, let's just sing the song. Trust me!" She then instructs, "Take him down to the judges." As Brynn walks towards the judges' table with the puppet, the puppet asks, "Is that allowed?" Judge 2 (woman in yellow top) and Judge 3 (man in dark suit) both respond with "Yeah, yeah!" The stage is lit with dynamic blue and gold, and the judges, including Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket), are seated behind a sleek table with 'X' designs and Sonic-branded cups. The audience is visibly engaged, clapping and smiling. Judge 2 leans forward, laughing, while Judge 3 raises his hand in astonishment. Brynn hands the puppet to Judge 2, who gently touches its fur. Judge 2, impressed, whispers, "He's so good," a sentiment echoed by Judge 3 who adds, "He's good." The puppet, now on the table, expresses worry in its high-pitched voice, "I have a bad feeling about this." Brynn, in her natural voice, reassures it, "Oh, stop worrying." The puppet replies, "Seriously, I don't know what I'm supposed to do." Brynn then suggests, "It's okay, we'll use a deck of cards. Check your pocket." The puppet reacts with a surprised "Wait, what? Ta-da!" Judge 2 picks up a red straw from her Sonic cup, intrigued. Brynn then addresses Judge 2 by name, "Heidi, I'd like you to cut the deck one time," as playful, slightly mysterious music plays in the background, building anticipation for the mentalism act.

The segment concludes with Judge 3 standing up, possibly to interact further, as the audience continues their enthusiastic applause.

The video segment opens with a close-up of Brynn Cummings, a young girl in a green dress, blindfolded and holding a pink and white furry puppet with large blue eyes. The scene cuts to the judges' table, where Judge 3 raises his arm as the puppet asks, "Sofia, what was your object?" The audience cheers and gasps as dramatic music swells, and the puppet declares, "Oh my gosh! Since you're doing so well, you're going to try this one solo. Penelope, come on, I need your help." The visibly engaged audience leans forward, laughing, as a wide shot shows Judges 1, 2, and 3 seated behind a modern table with 'X' designs and Sonic-branded cups, against a vibrant cityscape backdrop. Judge 3 then stands to place a black blindfold over Brynn’s eyes, and Judge 1, identified as Simon, responds, "Uh, yes." The audience erupts in loud cheers and applause as the music swells dramatically, with exclamations of "Wow! Oh my gosh! Wow!" The segment opens with Brynn Cummings, a young girl in a green dress with a white heart pattern, smiling warmly on stage, holding a pink and white furry puppet with large blue eyes against a dynamic, illuminated cityscape backdrop. event_blocks.summary: As Brynn Cummings, a young girl with her pink and white furry puppet, receives a standing ovation and four "yes" votes from judges Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie, Simon expresses his admiration, and Brynn declares it the best moment of her life before exiting through a red curtain. The show then introduces Mandy Muden, a British magician from London, who engages the judges with comedic banter about her motivations for being on "America's Got Talent" and her plans for prize money. Mandy proceeds to perform a magic trick involving a dictionary and audience-selected words, successfully guessing "fantasizing" from Sophia and "loony" from Howie, while Simon reveals his word was "eat," Heidi's was "a," and Sofia's was "t." The judges, including Simon, Heidi, and Howie, praise Mandy's performance, with Howie calling her the "epitome of somebody that deserves to have their own show." Mandy then receives four "yes" votes from the judges, eliciting an ecstatic reaction from her and the cheering audience, with the "America's Got Talent" logo and a vibrant cityscape backdrop illuminating the stage.

event_blocks.summary: Mandy Muden, a performer in a leopard-print jacket, expresses her gratitude and amazement on the America's Got Talent stage, calling her performance "amazing" and Judge Howie a "hero." The focus then shifts to Ryland, a 10-year-old magician from England, who confidently approaches the judges' table. He explains his act involves choosing friends from a group of young men in black shirts, each holding numbered cards, and having the judges select a friend and a number. Howie chooses 'Mark' and number 7 for the first position, while Heidi selects 'Om' and number 11 for the fourth position. Ryland then presents the judges with hand-drawn cards: a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains, which he reveals spell the magic word "scam." The judges and audience enthusiastically shout the word, and Ryland then reveals that the chosen drawings matched the cards they had in envelopes, astonishing everyone. He later confides his mission to get in and out of AGT quickly, expressing nervousness about Simon's reaction, before the judges reveal their matching "scam" cards, leading to Ryland's playful apology and a final wide shot of the stage and logo.

raw_segments: The segment opens with an upbeat, celebratory track as Mandy Muden, a woman with blonde hair in a leopard-print jacket and a large white statement necklace, walks confidently across the America's Got Talent stage, acknowledging the cheering and applauding audience. She approaches the judges' table, engaging in animated conversation with Judge 1, Simon, who is bald, wears glasses, and smiles from behind his 'Collins Pocket English Dictionary'. Mandy, speaking with an enthusiastic British accent, expresses her shock and calls her performance "amazing," adding that she "couldn't believe what Howie said," referring to Judge 3, and calling him "such a hero of mine." She profusely thanks the judges and audience as the music continues and the crowd claps. The scene then shifts to a young boy named Ryland, wearing a teal polo shirt and brown pants, standing center stage with a sheet of paper. Judge 2, Sofia (or Heidi), a woman in a yellow top, warmly welcomes him, asking his name, age, and origin. Ryland, appearing nervous yet focused, clearly states, "My name is Ryland," that he's "10 years old," and "from England," prompting a burst of cheers and applause from the engaged audience. Sofia acknowledges the crowd's reaction before asking, "What is your talent, young man?" Ryland, after a slight pause, declares, "I'm a magician," eliciting an "Ooh!" and "Wow!" from Sofia and renewed applause from the audience. He then reveals he's been practicing "since I was two, so eight years."

The segment opens with a wide shot of the America's Got Talent judges' panel, featuring Simon (bald, glasses, black jacket), Heidi (woman in yellow top), and Howie (man in dark suit), seated at a table with Sonic-branded cups and red buzzers. The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue and orange lighting, a futuristic cityscape backdrop, and the show's logo, while the engaged audience claps. Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo shirt, appears nervous yet focused as he walks to center stage, then confidently approaches the judges' table to hand a paper to Simon, who examines it with interest. The judges react with smiles and applause, Heidi clapping enthusiastically. Ryland returns to a music stand at center stage, as the audience cheers and the judges lean forward, intrigued. Close-ups show Simon smiling, Heidi clapping, and Howie nodding, building an electric atmosphere. Ryland begins, "I was born with magical powers and my dad's a magician," prompting applause. Heidi asks, "What about the million dollars? If you get it, what would you do with that?" Ryland replies, "I'll give some to my mom, my sister, and my dad and spend the rest on Legos," eliciting laughter and cheers. Heidi exclaims, "Okay, let's see what you've got tonight!" as dramatic orchestral music swells with loud audience applause. Ryland announces, "Tonight, I would like you to help me create a brand new magic word." Simon interjects, "A magic word!" Ryland continues, "But first, I have gifts. I've drawn you each a picture to look at later." He presents them, saying, "This one's for you, Howie," to which Howie responds, "Alright." He adds, "This one's for you, Heidi," and Heidi replies, "Thank you." Ryland then states, "This one's for you, Sofia, and this one's for you, Simon," concluding with, "Now, don't peek inside the envelopes. Don't let anyone else touch them," as the audience laughs.

The segment opens on the vibrant America's Got Talent stage, illuminated with blue and orange lights and featuring a cityscape backdrop, as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo and brown pants, confidently walks towards the judges' panel. He addresses the audience and judges, stating with a clear, articulate voice, "We'll get back to them later. What you're about to see is amazing, but I can't do it on my own. I need help. Luckily, I have lots of friends that love magic just like me." Judge 2, Heidi, in a yellow top, warmly exclaims, "Oh, hello there!" while Judge 3, Howie, in a dark suit, adds a friendly, "Hi friends." Ryland hands a paper to Judge 1, Simon, who smiles and reacts with interest. The camera then cuts to a group of young men in black shirts, bearing names like 'JAKE', 'MARK', 'JUSTIN', 'JORDAN', 'RYAN', 'OM', and 'SKYLER', each holding numbered cards. Ryland stands before them, explaining his act: "I'll let each of you to pick one of my friends, pick one of the numbers, and I'll place it in this holder. You get to choose which position it goes in. There are multiple combinations and the choices are all yours." He then directs Howie, "We'll start with you, Howie. Please name one of my friends." Howie, with a slight hesitation, responds, "Me? Uh, okay, uh, Mark." Ryland asks, "And what would you like, the number 7 or 23?" to which Howie confidently replies, "I'll take 7, it's lucky." When asked, "And where would you like it to go? 1, 2, 3 or 4?" Howie playfully states, "First, 'cause I'm first." Ryland acknowledges, "Okay, so number one. Heidi, your turn. Which of my friends?" Heidi, with a clear voice, chooses, "I choose Om." Ryland then offers, "Would you like 11 or 26?" and Heidi quickly decides, "11." For its placement, Ryland asks, "And where would you like that to go? 2, 3 or 4?" and Heidi responds, "Four." Ryland thanks her, "Thank you, Heidi. Sofia, it's your turn. Which of my friends would you like?" as he continues to direct the performance, placing numbers on a black music stand. The audience watches intently, clapping and smiling, while the judges remain engaged, with Judge 2 and Judge 3 showing amusement and curiosity, and Judge 1 listening attentively.

The America's Got Talent stage glows with its iconic logo and a vibrant cityscape backdrop as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo, confidently directs a group of young men in black shirts, each holding numbered cards, from a black music stand. Ryland's clear, enthusiastic voice asks, "Skyler! Would you like 24 or 10?" to which a group member responds, "10!" He then asks, "And where would you like it to go? Two or three?" and the reply is "Two!" After thanking "Sophia," Ryland turns to the judges, asking Judge 3, "And Simon, which of my friends would you like?" Judge 3, identified as Simon Cowell by his distinct British voice, replies, "Jordan." Ryland repeats, "Jordan," then asks, "Would you like one or 15?" Simon chooses, "One." Ryland proposes a "different" task, asking if he'd like to put it in the last position or swap it, to which Simon responds, "Okay," with a slight chuckle. Judge 1, identified as Howie Mandel by his voice, interjects humorously, "He's taking my position," which Simon echoes with a laugh, as the audience erupts in laughter and applause. Ryland thanks Simon, and the applause continues. He then shifts focus, saying, "Now, let's look at the drawings you didn't use," describing them as "all different" and listing "Lego, Dragons, We've even got burgers." Simon confirms with a "Yes." Ryland then presents the chosen hand-drawn cards, identifying them as "A snake," "A slice of lime," "A house," and "Some mountains," as the judges watch intently, with Simon pointing and engaging, and the lively audience continues to clap amidst the upbeat, suspenseful background music.

The video opens with a close-up of Judge 2 (woman in yellow top) smiling broadly at the judges' table, as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo shirt, stands center stage. He gestures towards a black music stand displaying four colorful hand-drawn cards: a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains. Ryland's clear, articulate voice explains, "Each of you had a free choice of which drawing you wanted, and more importantly, where to put it." He then details how "The snake looks like an S, the lime looks like a C, the house looks like an A, and the mountains look like an M," concluding, "That spells the word scam." Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket) confirms with a verbal "Right?", and Judge 3 (man in dark suit) adds a "Yeah," as Ryland declares, "That can be our magic word," prompting laughter from the audience and judges. Ryland then instructs, "Everyone shout scam as loud as they can on the count of three. One, two, three!" The audience and judges loudly exclaim "SCAM!" followed by applause and laughter. Ryland continues, "But what if I told you that we knew what the magic word would be before you even picked it?" He instructs the judges to take their drawings "out of the envelope and show it to everyone." As upbeat, suspenseful music begins to play, Judge 1, Judge 2, and Judge 3 are seen holding up their matching cards—snake, lime, house, and mountains—to an audible "Ooh!" from the audience. Ryland confirms, "As you can see, they match," as the stage, illuminated with dynamic blue and orange lighting and a large star-shaped backdrop, is filled with the sound of enthusiastic applause and cheers from the engaged audience, clearly enjoying the performance.

The segment opens with enthusiastic audience applause and upbeat music as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo shirt, confidently directs a group of young men in black shirts on the America's Got Talent stage. They are seen arranging numbered cards on a black music stand against a vibrant cityscape backdrop. Ryland's clear voice explains his act, declaring, "Word equals scam," which elicits an amused "Wow!" from Judge Sofia. The scene then cuts to a close-up of Ryland, where he conspiratorially explains his mission: "Okay, so I'm at AGT. I'm gonna go in, do what I need to do, get out as fast as I can," expressing his disbelief at being in the AGT theater. Back on stage, Judge Howie, watching from the judges' table with Simon and Heidi, asks, "What is he doing?" as Ryland whispers, "Oh god, Simon's gonna hate this." Ryland then directly addresses Judge Simon, stating, "Now, you're probably wondering what I did at the desk, Simon. Well, hopefully you're not too mad. Watch this." As Ryland walks toward the judges, they reveal colorful hand-drawn cards—a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains—which were previously arranged on the music stand. Judge Simon exclaims, "Whoa, it's a scam everywhere," as Ryland rapidly emphasizes, "Scam, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam, scam!" The lively and playful atmosphere, with the audience cheering and the judges actively participating, concludes with Ryland playfully apologizing, "Sorry," as a wide shot shows the stage, the judges' table with Sonic-branded cups, and the illuminated America's Got Talent logo.

event_blocks.summary: Magic Mike Jacobson, a magician in a mustard-yellow jacket, captivated the America's Got Talent audience and judges with a series of card tricks. He began by interacting with the judges, including Simon Cowell, Heidi Klum, and Howie Mandel, explaining his background and admitting he works in advertising by day. During his performance, he presented an 'invisible deck of cards' and engaged the judges in a trick involving a lit matchstick, where they chose to 'burn' face cards and then 'men' from the deck. The climax involved Howie Mandel shuffling the deck, picking a card, and later being astonished when Magic Mike revealed the Jack of Diamonds, which Howie had signed with an 'invisible Sharpie.' The judges, particularly Howie and Heidi, expressed amazement and praised the act as 'incredible,' 'fantastic,' and 'funny.' The performance concluded with all four judges giving Magic Mike Jacobson a 'yes,' securing his advancement in the competition.

Judge 3 (man in dark suit).description: A man in a black long-sleeve shirt, seated at the judges' table, speaking into the microphone with expressive hand movements, showing surprise and curiosity as he reacts to the performance. Oh my god!', asking 'How are you feeling? Tell me,' asserting 'That's my signature, too' and 'It is my signature,' and agreeing 'It is crazy.'

She is heard saying 'I say yes for me too,' confirming her vote with 'Yes!', and commenting 'That is crazy' and 'That's crazy, Tom' in reaction to the trick.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a dynamic scene from America's Got Talent, as a group of young performers in black shirts, some with names like 'JAKE' and 'OM', run excitedly across the stage, met by an eruption of cheers and a resounding 'Whoa!' from the enthusiastic audience. The camera cuts to the judges' table, where Judge 1, identified by his nameplate as 'SIMON' (bald, glasses, black jacket), reacts with surprise and amusement, while Judge 2 (woman in yellow top) and Judge 3 (man in dark suit) are also visibly engaged amidst the continued applause. The focus then shifts to a new performer, Magic Mike Jacobson, who walks confidently onto the stage in a mustard-yellow jacket over a white t-shirt and black pants. He stands center stage, hands clasped, and greets the audience with a 'Hello, hello.' Judge 1 (Simon) immediately asks, 'What's your name?', to which Magic Mike Jacobson replies, 'Magic Mike Jacobson.' Judge 1 (Simon) repeats his name, then asks, 'So you're gonna be doing magic?' Magic Mike Jacobson confirms, 'That's right,' adding that the movie 'was loosely based on my life' after Judge 1 (Simon) comments on the coincidence of his name. The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue and gold lighting, featuring a large star-shaped backdrop with the America's Got Talent logo. Judge 1 (Simon) continues to engage him, asking, 'How long have you been doing magic?' Magic Mike Jacobson explains he's 'did magic everywhere I could, from like my home to barber shops and everything in between,' and that it 'rose and rose and rose.' Judge 1 (Simon) then inquires about his age and origin, to which Magic Mike Jacobson responds, '36' and 'LA.' When asked if he makes a living doing magic, Magic Mike Jacobson admits, 'Uh, no, uh, not yet. I work in advertising by day, but um, this performance will...' as the segment concludes, with him maintaining eye contact with the judges and audience, and the stage lights reflecting off the glossy floor, enhancing the theatrical ambiance.

The segment opens with a wide shot of the America's Got Talent judges' table, featuring Judge 1 (Simon, bald, glasses, black jacket), Judge 2 (Heidi, woman in yellow top), and Judge 3 (Sofia, man in dark suit), seated behind a sleek white table adorned with Sonic-branded cups and red buzzers, all against a vibrant cityscape backdrop displaying the show’s logo. Upbeat, suspenseful music plays as Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard, wearing a mustard-yellow jacket, stands center stage, using expressive hand gestures as he addresses the audience, his voice slightly nervous but confident as he states, "we'll see what happens." Judge 2 (Heidi) lightheartedly interjects, "No pressure!", to which Magic Mike responds, "Yeah, no big deal. But actually a huge deal for me," admitting he's "a little nervous" but "so excited to be here." Judge 1 (Simon) encourages him, asking, "Are you nervous?" and wishing him "the best of luck, buddy," prompting cheers and applause from the captivated audience. Magic Mike then walks confidently toward the judges, engaging them with animated movements, explaining he will share something "every magician has, but no magician will show you." He reveals an "invisible deck of cards," acknowledging it's "a bit hard to see in this light," and announces his plan to "burn the invisible deck from 52 all the way down to one." As he approaches, he performs a magic trick, producing a small red and blue object from his hand, which he hands to Judge 1 (Simon). The judges react with curiosity and amusement, leaning forward to examine the object, with Judge 3 (Sofia) showing a focused expression and Judge 2 (Heidi) smiling. Magic Mike then directly addresses Judge 1 (Simon), saying, "Simon, you're going to be a hero," and mentions that the outcome "depends on what Heidi and Sofia decide," instructing Judge 2 (Heidi) to "take that match." The stage lighting shifts dynamically, highlighting the performer and the judges, while the America's Got Talent logo remains prominent. The segment ends with Magic Mike Jacobson standing beside the judges’ table, continuing his performance with a flourish, as the audience remains captivated, their excitement audible.

The segment opens with a close-up of Judge 2, a woman in a bright yellow off-the-shoulder top with long blonde hair and sparkling jewelry, holding a lit matchstick near her face with a focused, slightly amused expression as suspenseful, upbeat music plays. Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, stands on a stage with a vibrant, illuminated cityscape backdrop, gesturing expressively as he performs a magic trick. He approaches the judges' table, where Judge 2, Judge 3 (a man in a dark suit), Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket), and Judge 3's counterpart (a woman in a red dress) are seated with Sonic-branded cups. Magic Mike explains, "Let's just say there's two groups, number cards and face cards. We're going to decide whether you want to start to burn all the numbers or all the faces." He instructs Judge 2, "Blow it out when you're ready." Judge 2 asks, "Am I telling you?" to which Magic Mike replies, "Yes, please." She then declares, "All the faces," and blows out the matchstick flame, receiving a small red and blue object from Magic Mike. He states, "The faces are now in the fire. You can pass the box over to Sophia." The camera cuts to the woman in the red dress, who also holds a lit matchstick and blows it out with a smile after Magic Mike tells her, "Sophia, take your match." He continues, "Left in the fire, we got Jacks, Queens, Kings. The only way to divide those up would be, yeah, men and women. Only to burn that match and tell us, do you want to burn all the men, do you want to burn all the women to a crisp?" She quickly responds, "Oh, the men." Magic Mike exclaims, "That was a very quick decision! Go ahead and you blow it out. And you can pass that to Simon. You're going to save one of the remaining cards for burning. All we have left are the four Queens." He lists, "Queen of Hearts, Queen of Spades, Queen of Clubs, Queen of..." as the audience watches intently, some clapping and smiling, before erupting in applause and cheers as Magic Mike smiles and gestures toward them.

The segment opens with Magic Mike Jacobson, a magician in a mustard-yellow jacket, performing a close-up magic trick on a stage lit with vibrant blue and orange hues, featuring a star-shaped backdrop and cityscape design. He begins by holding a small object, instructing the judges, "We're going to light that match," which is then lit with a distinct sound. He tells Judge 2, a woman in a yellow top, "And whenever you're ready, blow it out," followed by a blowing sound. Magic Mike then directs Judge 3, a man in a dark suit, "I want you to draw with that match the suit of the queen that you want to save. Hearts, spades, clubs, or diamonds," as he performs a series of sleight-of-hand movements. The camera cuts between his dexterous hand movements and the judges' reactions, particularly Judge 3 (Simon), who watches intently. Magic Mike asks, "Is that a diamond? Here's the thing, are you sure?" He then addresses Judge 3 by name, "Simon, if you shake the matchbox, do you hear something in there?" A shaking sound is heard as Judge 3 complies. Magic Mike instructs, "Open that up for us," explaining, "Because in all of your hands the entire time, there's been nothing but one card sitting inside there, protected by a paperclip." He reveals a playing card, the Jack of Diamonds, manipulating it skillfully, while ultimately declaring the trick was to "save the Queen of Diamonds." He continues, "I want you to make sure it's singed, like it's been at a bit of a fire. You guys could have saved whatever you wanted to, but you and you and you decided together to save the Queen of Diamonds." The judges, including Judge 3 and Judge 2, show expressions of amazement and amusement, while the audience claps and smiles, their engagement culminating in enthusiastic applause and cheers at the trick's reveal. Upbeat, suspenseful theatrical music underscores the entire performance, building tension and excitement. The segment ends with Magic Mike holding the card and gesturing toward the captivated judges, concluding with a "Thank you."

The segment opens with a wide shot of an enthusiastic audience clapping and cheering under vibrant blue and orange stage lighting, with a dynamic cityscape backdrop featuring the 'America's Got Talent' logo. Upbeat music plays as Magic Mike Jacobson, in a mustard-yellow jacket, addresses the judges, saying "Thank you very much." He then engages Judge 1, identified as Howie, asking him to shuffle an "invisible deck of cards" right next to his Sonic-branded cup, prompting audience laughter. Judge 1 (Howie), a bald man with glasses in a black jacket, speaks animatedly and gestures, while Judge 2 (Heidi), a woman in a yellow top, listens intently, her expression shifting from curiosity to amusement. Judge 3 (Simon), in a dark suit, smiles and gestures. As Magic Mike instructs Howie to "take it out of the box first," more audience laughter erupts, and Howie makes a sound of amusement. Magic Mike continues, asking Howie to "show off all your shuffling skills," leading to audience gasps, cheers, and applause as the upbeat music swells. Judge 2 (Heidi) exclaims, "Oh wow!" and Judge 3 (Simon) adds, "That was very impressive," while Judge 1 (Howie) declares, "It's so good!" Judge 2 (Heidi) then praises, "Howie's a pro!" The camera pans across the engaged audience, including a woman in a blue blazer and another in a coral blazer, both smiling and clapping. Magic Mike then instructs Howie to fan out the deck and pick a card, asking him to "sign the back of the card for me, just with your first name" using an "invisible Sharpie" next to his cup, eliciting more audience laughter. The segment concludes with Judge 1 (Howie) pressing his red buzzer, its distinct sound cutting through the continued audience cheers and applause, as the lively, theatrical atmosphere with dynamic lighting and a sense of anticipation fills the theater.

The video opens with Judge 1, a bald man with glasses and a black jacket, adjusting his microphone and gesturing, as Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, begins a close-up magic trick on stage. Magic Mike's voice is heard instructing, "Now, take the card, slide it right back in. Put that all back in the box. And then throw it right back up to me whenever you're ready. Perfect." He skillfully shuffles a deck of cards, drawing the judges' attention. The audience reacts with cheers and applause, prompting Magic Mike to playfully remark, "Now, you... just just minor miracles, it's fine." He then addresses Judge 1 by name, "Howie, you shuffled the deck. You picked a card, you all saw it. For the very first time. What's the card that you picked?" Judge 1, identified as Howie, responds with a clear voice, "Ten of Clubs." Magic Mike clarifies, "I want to clarify something. You and I have never met, correct? Never. Nobody came up to you before the show, said anything? For the very first time when I open this up, you said the Ten of Clubs, correct?" Howie confirms, "I did." Magic Mike builds suspense, explaining, "Because every single card in here is face up, except for one." Dramatic, suspenseful music swells as Judge 2, a woman in a yellow top, reacts with a soft "Uh-oh." Magic Mike reveals a single card, the Jack of Diamonds, holding it up and asking Howie, "One card with a signature on it. Howie, can you confirm, is that your signature?" Howie, surprised, exclaims, "It is." Magic Mike then asks, "Will you flip that over for me?" to which Howie responds with disbelief and excitement, "Are you kidding me?" Magic Mike delivers the final line, "Is that your card?" The audience erupts in massive cheers, applause, and gasps. Magic Mike thanks them, "Thank you very much," as the vibrant blue and orange stage lighting and cityscape backdrop enhance the theatrical atmosphere, with Sonic-branded cups visible on the judges' table.

The video opens with a close-up of a hand holding a playing card with an intricate blue and white design, hinting at a magic trick, as an excited voice exclaims "crazy!" The scene quickly transitions to a wide shot of the vibrant 'America's Got Talent' stage, its iconic logo illuminated, with the judges' table visible. Upbeat, celebratory music swells as the audience claps enthusiastically under dynamic blue and orange lighting. Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, stands center stage, appearing humble and grateful, repeatedly saying "Thank you, thank you" to the roaring crowd. Judge Howie Mandel, in a dark suit, humorously remarks, "And he kept all his clothes on!" eliciting laughter, before asking Judge Heidi Klum, in a yellow top, for her thoughts. Heidi, gesturing with surprise, describes how "the audience was so quiet," imitating gasps of amazement, and declaring the act "incredible." Mike thanks her, and Judge Simon Cowell, bald with glasses, adds a concise "Really cool." Heidi continues, expressing how she was "mesmerized" and found the trick "fantastic" and "funny at the beginning also with the invisible part." Howie chimes in with "Good job!" and Mike again thanks them. Howie then makes a prediction, stating, "No more advertising jobs for you. Because this is what I think you were born to do," genuinely adding, "Thank you." Simon enthusiastically shouts "Yes!" as the audience erupts in cheers. Mike expresses his gratitude with "Thank you so much." Howie further emphasizes the trick's authenticity, telling the audience, "I swear to you, nobody here was in on this. This is my signature," as Mike holds up the Jack of Diamonds for all to see. Simon begins to speak, "That was...", as the segment concludes with Mike bowing slightly, acknowledging the electric atmosphere and continued applause.

The segment opens with the visibly excited audience clapping enthusiastically, setting a lively atmosphere.
